recommendation: Tie rehearsal to the user's real schedule, priority task, and concrete first step.
Keep tone supportive and low-pressure; avoid hype that makes the day feel larger.
Use a predictable progression: check in, name priority, rehearse steps, close with next action.
Start from current body/energy state, then return to a body cue after visualization.
Support spoken/text guidance with optional calm background audio.
Use imagery when it clarifies the user's next behavior, not as generic fantasy.
Offer a short default with optional expansion; many users want guidance, not a long essay.
Include one likely obstacle and one realistic recovery move.
Connect work to values briefly, then turn that value into visible action.
